title: STAR Incident - Cognizant Data Quality and Model Underperformance
content:
Incident: Early contract model underperformance driven by data issues

Situation:
- Initial model iterations for contract workflows showed weak gains despite complex modeling attempts.
- Dataset imbalance and noisy inputs limited practical performance.

Task:
- Recover model effectiveness by diagnosing root causes and stabilizing the training and feature pipeline.

Action:
- Performed data distribution and quality analysis to identify imbalance and noise patterns.
- Prioritized data cleaning, stratified splitting, and feature engineering over model complexity escalation.
- Re-established baselines with simpler models before reintroducing advanced methods.
- Tuned pipeline based on measured error patterns and domain-specific signal quality.

Result:
- Source narrative indicates meaningful accuracy recovery and stronger production readiness.
- Incident reinforced a data-first debugging approach for enterprise ML workflows.

metadata.source: career-source career overview + cognizant deep dive
